title: Varied market as VLCCs bask in storage interest
section_path: Varied market as VLCCs bask in storage interest
section_path_text: Varied market as VLCCs bask in storage interest
summary:
VLCCs
Rates in the Middle East Gulf have held around WS 67.50-70 level for 270,000 tonnes to South Korea/China. For trades going west Bahri managed to cover 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf on two ships early this week at WS 35/36 (cape/cape basis), but later in the week Litasco reportedly took Athenian tonnage from Basrah for west options only at WS 42 (cape/cape), although they would have paid some premium for the options. The owners' cause is still being helped by ongoing storage interest with rates varying from $42,500 daily to rumoured high $40,000s.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: Rates in the Middle East Gulf have held around WS 67.50-70 level for 270,000 tonnes to South Korea/China. For trades going west Bahri managed to cover 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf on two ships early this week at WS 35/36 (cape/cape basis), but later in the week Litasco reportedly took Athenian tonnage from Basrah for west options only at WS 42 (cape/cape), although they would have paid some premium for the options.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: Rates for West Africa to Continent after initially holding at WS 92.5 came under pressure as tonnage numbers increased. Consequently Total were able to conclude at a lower WS 88.75 although it is now believed this deal may have failed on subjects.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, an active start to the week saw rates firm considerably with the week's high being WS 165, although this was for Libya load which incurs a premium due to the current political problems. Thereafter levels peaked at around WS 140 and rates are now coming under pressure as suezmaxes struggle and seek coverage also by playing the aframax market accordingly.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: It has been a good week for panamax owners with levels being fixed around WS 155 for 55,000 tonne cargoes from Continent to the US Gulf and ice business from Baltic/transatlantic paying WS 170 plus.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: It has been a disappointing week for owners as a lack of enquiry combined with a build-up of tonnage has seen levels for MR tonnage fall from WS 142.5 at the start of the week to barely WS 130 and lower levels likely. The 38,000 tonne backhaul route from US Gulf to UK-Cont has also struggled with rates easing from WS 92.5 down to low WS 80s.
